title: Ouratea lanceolata
intro: Ouratea lanceolata, also known as the "Ouratea plant", is a species of flowering plant in the family Ochnaceae, native to the tropical regions of Central and South America. The Ouratea plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine to treat various health issues, including fever, rheumatism, and digestive problems, due to its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The bioactive constituents of Ouratea lanceolata include flavonoids, alkaloids, and phenolic acids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In herbal medicine, Ouratea lanceolata is commonly prepared as a tea or infusion, and is also used to make tinctures and extracts, which are used to treat a range of health conditions.

Further classification of Ouratea lanceolata is as a genus within the Ochnaceae family.
